evidence: docs/README.md says no canonical docs/verification or release-evidence route exists yet and assigns bounded proof ownership to HGI-203 and operational routing to HGI-204.
packages/scripts/README.md records command stdout and stderr in an invocation outcome but does not define full-log retention, redaction, canonical storage, access control, or deletion policy.
The source receipt found no canonical release-proof or runbook route.
rejectedAlternatives: Retain raw command logs or secrets as durable release proof.
Treat provider CI logs as durable canonical proof.
Treat a local check as publication, deployment, registry, production, or external-consumer proof.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: The future canonical release-proof route is bounded sanitized JSON receipts under docs/evidence/releases/**. Receipts contain only commit, package, digest, and check evidence; raw logs and secrets are prohibited. Accepted receipts remain in Git history until explicitly superseded. Provider CI logs are transient/default and are not durable proof.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: A local check outcome is not publication, deployment, registry, production, or external-consumer proof.
Runbooks must use sanitized JSON receipts and must not retain raw logs or secrets.
Automation may enforce receipt shape and reject raw-log or secret retention; provider logs remain transient.

authorityImpact: Release and verification maintainers accepted the receipt route and retention boundary. This record grants no release, provider, registry, publication, deployment, or destructive evidence-operation authority.
nonClaims: No raw release logs, secrets, provider logs, publication, registry, deployment, production, or external-consumer evidence is retained by this policy.
No release, publication, registry, deployment, production, or external-consumer state is proved.
carryingCost: HGI-203 must define the bounded receipt schema and HGI-204 the operational procedure without expanding the approved data class.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: A change to receipt fields, Git-history retention, or any raw/provider log handling requires a superseding maintainer decision.
